name: RAGTruth
venue: ACL 2024 (62nd Annual Meeting of the Association for Computational Linguistics)
authors: Cheng Niu (NewsBreak / University of Illinois Urbana-Champaign), Yuanhao Wu, Juno Zhu, Siliang Xu, KaShun Shum, Randy Zhong, Juntong Song (NewsBreak); Tong Zhang (UIUC)
dataset_size: 17,838 responses across 2,973 source instances (nearly 18,000 responses total); 14,781 annotated hallucination spans; test set: 450 instances (150 per task)
annotation_unit: Word-level (span) annotations: each hallucination is marked with precise character start and end positions within the response text, making this span-level annotation at sub-sentence granularity. Also includes response-level binary labels (hallucinated or not).
ground_truth_source: Human annotation by professional annotators with bachelor's degrees in relevant fields. Two independent annotators per response; disagreements resolved by a third adjudicator. Response-level inter-annotator agreement: 91.8%; span-level: 78.8%. Used Label Studio annotation interface.
judge_model: None — fully human-annotated. No LLM judge used for ground truth.
domain: Three RAG task types: (1) Question Answering — general-knowledge QA using retrieved web passages (MS-MARCO-style); (2) Summarization — CNN/Daily Mail news summarization and recent news summarization; (3) Data-to-text — structured data (e.g., product, table) verbalization. Domains span news, general knowledge, and product/structured data.
context_length_range: 194–2,632 tokens (mean 801, median 741 per LettuceDetect analysis). Per-task context ranges: QA mean 243 tokens (max 509), CNN/DM summarization mean 648 tokens (max 1,749), recent news summarization mean 369 tokens (max 481), data-to-text mean 354 tokens (max 1,253). Short-to-medium context RAG setting; not long-doc.
error_taxonomy: Four hallucination types: (1) Evident Conflict — direct contradiction with source material, easily verifiable; (2) Subtle Conflict — contextual divergence altering intended meaning, requires deeper understanding; (3) Evident Introduction of Baseless Information — fabricated details with no source evidence; (4) Subtle Introduction of Baseless Information — inferred details or subjective assumptions beyond source. Additional metadata flags: 'due_to_null' (hallucination caused by missing source data), 'implicit_true' (correct info not in context but factually true).
contamination_risk: medium — test set is static (released publicly on GitHub); responses generated by GPT-3.5/GPT-4/Llama-2/Mistral in 2023, so newer LLMs may have memorized the source passages (CNN/DM, MS-MARCO). Source passages are from widely-used datasets.
annotation_cost_per_sample: [uncertain] — not reported in the paper. Two annotators + one adjudicator for 17,838 responses with word-level spans suggests significant cost. Response-level annotation is faster; span-level requires careful reading. No dollar figure published.
reported_value: Fine-tuned Llama-2-13B achieves F1 ~0.53 on span detection, competitive with GPT-4 prompt-based approaches
overlapping_benchmark: None directly (RAGTruth uses QA and summarization, not MATH-500/GSM8K/GPQA/HotpotQA/TriviaQA/WebQ as defined in our project)
model_used_in_paper: GPT-3.5-turbo-0613, GPT-4-0613, Llama-2-7B-chat, Llama-2-13B-chat, Llama-2-70B-chat (4-bit), Mistral-7B-Instruct for response generation; Llama-2-13B fine-tuned for detection
applicability: high — de-facto standard span-level RAG hallucination benchmark; 18k responses with human word-level labels; covers QA and summarization; MIT license; directly usable for long-doc RAG pilot evaluation
preemption_risk: low — RAGTruth is a benchmark/corpus, not a detection method. It does not use spectral features, eigenvalues, frequency-domain analysis, or entropy trajectories. Zero overlap with our spectral approach.
bracha_alignment: Moderate — the binary response-level labels are suitable for conformal prediction (LTT/COIN/PPI calibration). The span-level annotations could be used for conformal set prediction at the span level. Bracha's methods could calibrate detection thresholds on this benchmark with coverage guarantees.
ofir_alignment: Low — RAGTruth is a data corpus, not a method. No spectral or kernel machinery involved. Relevant only as an evaluation testbed for Ofir's spectral detection methods.
notes: RAGTruth is the most widely adopted span-level RAG hallucination benchmark as of 2024-2025. Key limitation: context lengths are short (mean 801 tokens), so it does not test long-doc RAG scenarios. The data-to-text split has an unusually high hallucination rate (69.2%), partly due to null-value handling. A re-annotation project (RAGTruth++) found ~10x more hallucination spans in a 408-sample QA/summarization subset, suggesting the original has significant false-negative rate for harder hallucination types.
model_size_range: 7B–70B (Llama-2 family) + GPT-3.5/GPT-4 for generation; 13B for fine-tuned detection
gpu_requirements: Fine-tuning detection model: 4x A100 GPUs, LoRA on Llama-2-13B, 3 epochs. Inference only: single GPU sufficient for 7B/13B models.
sample_size_for_replication: 450 (test set: 150 per task) for evaluation; full 17,838 responses for fine-tuning
min_samples_for_replication: 450 (the released test split) — sufficient for a 1-day pilot evaluation without fine-tuning
runtime_estimate: [uncertain] — not explicitly reported. Inference with 13B model on 17,838 short responses (mean 131 tokens) should be fast. Fine-tuning 3 epochs on 4x A100 reported but wall-clock time not stated.
cumulative_state_modeling: N/A — single-turn RAG responses, no multi-step trajectory
